header: S. S. da Grama (SP) / Pocos de Caldas (MG)

lesson.menu_desc: Reservations & Bookings -- Reported speech (said/told, backshift) -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Making Plans -- Reservations & Bookings
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Tuca Dias | Aula 14 -- Making Plans

listenings.text: Good afternoon, this is a message from the Garden View Hotel about your booking for next month. We are calling to confirm your reservation for two nights, from the fifteenth to the seventeenth. The receptionist asked me to tell you that your check-in time is three in the afternoon, and check-out is at eleven in the morning. She also said that we still have good availability for an early arrival, so if you arrive before three, please call us first. One important point: we received your deposit of fifty dollars, and the rest of the payment is due at check-in. The deadline to cancel without a fee is seven days before your arrival. If you cancel after that, we keep the deposit. The manager told me to remind you to bring your voucher and a photo ID to the front desk. If your plans change, just send us a message and we will update your reservation. We look forward to welcoming you. Thank you, and have a great trip.

Hi Tuca, it is your friend again. I just called the tour office about our day trip, so I am passing on what they said. The woman told me that the morning tour was already full, but she said there was still space on the afternoon one. She asked me to confirm the number of people by Friday, because that is the deadline. I told her that there would be two of us, you and me. She also said that we had to pay a small deposit online to hold the booking, and that the rest was due on the day. Oh, and she explained that if we cancelled more than two days before, we would get a full refund, but after that, no refund. So here is my advice: if I were you, I would book today and not wait. Send me a message when you have paid your half, and I will forward the voucher to your email. Talk soon. Bye.

extra_audio.text: First, Tuca went online to check the availability for the dates she wanted. Then she called the hotel and made a reservation for two nights. After that, she paid a small deposit to confirm the booking. Next, the hotel sent her a voucher by email with the check-in time. Finally, she saved the cancellation deadline in her phone, just in case her plans changed.
